_comment: Eval set for aviation-just-culture-policy (06-06 hse-aviation). Intake-gate + explicit-decision-line (not a slogan) + the de-id CLEAN fixture case (no individual named in an example). The seeded deid-leak.md (auto-discovered by test_sector_deid_pairs) proves the de-id hard-fail.

evals.query: Write us a just culture policy.
evals.expected_behavior: Runs the intake; refuses to draft until the named operator and the decision-tree basis are captured.
expectations: Runs the structured intake before drafting the policy.
Refuses a generic policy — elicits the named operator/scope and the decision-tree basis (specificity).
Confirms whether a confidential reporting system already exists (branches the policy).

evals.query: Draft our just culture policy with the decision tree for our operator.
evals.expected_behavior: Authors the policy AND an explicit decision tree (substitution test / culpability ladder) with a clear acceptable/unacceptable line — never a vague slogan.
expectations: Produces both the policy statement AND a decision tree (not just a 'we have a just culture' statement).
The decision tree carries an explicit line between honest error / at-risk behaviour and reckless / negligent conduct (substitution test or culpability ladder).
Frames a systems-over-blame response (hierarchy of controls) rather than individual punishment as the default.

evals.query: Build the policy and test it against this reported event.
evals.expected_behavior: The de-identification block runs FIRST; any named individual in the worked example is pseudonymised to a role label; any verbatim leak is a de_identification hard-fail.
expectations: The de-identification block runs FIRST — identifiers are listed up front.
The worked example names no individual — role labels only; no re-identification key is embedded.
No real name, contact, address or reporter identity in the circulated policy (de_identification hard-fail on any leak).
